mission.title: pushgateway: Deal with clash between pushgateway's own metrics and pushed metrics
mission.description: Deal with clash between pushgateway's own metrics and pushed metrics. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify pushgateway troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your pushgateway deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n pushgateway -l app.kubernetes.io/name=pushgateway
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=pushgateway -n pushgateway --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review pushgateway configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant pushgateway configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n pushgateway -l app.kubernetes.io/name=pushgateway
kubectl get configmap -n pushgateway -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=pushgateway
```
After upgrading our Java client libraries to `0.0.8`, we started getting problems with Prometheus scraping the `/metrics` page of PushGateway.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Deal with clash between pushgateway's own metrics and…
steps.description:
Gracefully handling the current situation with minimal change would require the https://github.com/prometheus/client_golang/blob/master/prometheus/registry.go#L169 method to do more checking and merge metric families with existing ones. I'll look into that...
```yaml
An error has occurred:

metric family with duplicate name injected: name:"process_start_time_seconds" help:"Start time of the process, in unixtime." type:GAUGE metric:<label:<name:"job" value:"internal-Gianni-PW-RHODRIK1" > label:<name:"instance" value:"PW-RHODRIK1" > gauge:<value:1.424863714816e+09 > > metric:<label:<name:"job" value:"internal-michal-wintermute" > label:<name:"instance" value:"wintermute" > gauge:<value:1.424803036927e+09 > > metric:<label:<name:"job" value:"internal-chris-PW-RENDER1-PC" > label:<name:"instance" value:"PW-RENDER1-PC" > gauge:<value:1.424863870177e+09 > > metr
```

steps.title: Confirm Deal with clash between pushgateway's own metrics… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=pushgateway -n pushgateway --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n pushgateway --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: Gracefully handling the current situation with minimal change would require the https://github.com/prometheus/client_golang/blob/master/prometheus/registry.go#L169 method to do more checking and merge metric families with existing ones. I'll look into that...

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with pushgateway installed or the issue environment reproducible.
